what_it_does: Declares a single 'contradiction' mark-type record as a Python dictionary constant, specifying its id, value shape, direction, and description for a ledger of code/design marks.
observations: The file defines MARK_TYPE with id 'contradiction', value_shape 'span', and direction 'surface'
The description explicitly says the operator decides; the system renders, not judges
The docstring frames contradiction as a relational disposition written by a mark-pass
It notes that the mark id must equal the file stem ('contradiction')
No executable logic or symbols are present beyond the constant and docstring
standouts: Direction is 'surface' — it exposes tension for review rather than subtracting noise
Value shape is 'span', pointing to a claim or span that contradicts another
The description defers judgment to the operator
notes: This file seeds a single mark-type record that surfaces contradictions as operator-reviewable tensions rather than automated defects.
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it defines the contradiction mark-type with the declared span shape and surface direction.
intention_signals: To model contradiction as a first-class, review-oriented relation in a mark ledger, where the system exposes the tension and defers resolution to a human operator.
novelty: It treats contradiction as a surfaced tension for review rather than an error or noise to suppress automatically.
contribution: Provides a concrete mark-type definition for contradiction relations, capturing a span-shaped value, surface direction, and an explicit deferral of judgment to the operator.
summary_for_embedding: company/mark_types/contradiction.py defines the 'contradiction' mark-type as a span-shaped, surface-direction ledger record meant to expose inter-unit contradictions for operator review.
intention_for_embedding: To seed a non-judgmental, operator-resolved mark-type for recording contradictions between units in a code/design ledger.
